verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est, cum consummasset Jesus sermones istos, migravit a Galilæa, et venit in fines Judææ trans Jordanem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ὅτε ἐτέλεσεν ὁ ἰησοῦς τοὺς λόγους τούτους μετῆρεν ἀπὸ τῆς γαλιλαίας καὶ ἦλθεν εἰς τὰ ὅρια τῆς ἰουδαίας πέραν τοῦ ἰορδάνου
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, *that* when Jesus had finished these sayings, he departed from Galilee, and came into the coasts of Judaea beyond Jordan;

verses.latin_vulgate: et secutæ sunt eum turbæ multæ, et curavit eos ibi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῷ ὄχλοι πολλοί καὶ ἐθεράπευσεν αὐτοὺς ἐκεῖ
verses.king_james: And great multitudes followed him; and he healed them there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accesserunt ad eum pharisæi tentantes eum, et dicentes: Si licet homini dimittere uxorem suam, quacumque ex causa?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσῆλθον αὐτῷ οἱ φαρισαῖοι πειράζοντες αὐτὸν καὶ λέγοντες αὐτῷ εἰ ἔξεστιν ἀνθρώπῳ ἀπολῦσαι τὴν γυναῖκα αὐτοῦ κατὰ πᾶσαν αἰτίαν
verses.king_james: The Pharisees also came unto him, tempting him, and saying unto him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife for every cause?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui respondens, ait eis: Non legistis, quia qui fecit hominem ab initio, masculum et feminam fecit eos? Et dixit:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς οὐκ ἀνέγνωτε ὅτι ὁ ποιήσας ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς ἄρσεν καὶ θῆλυ ἐποίησεν αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And he answered and said unto them, Have ye not read, that he which made *them* at the beginning made them male and female,

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter hoc dimittet homo patrem, et matrem, et adhærebit uxori suæ, et erunt duo in carne una.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν ἕνεκεν τούτου καταλείψει ἄνθρωπος τὸν πατέρα καὶ τὴν μητέρα καὶ προσκολληθήσεται τῇ γυναικὶ αὐτοῦ καὶ ἔσονται οἱ δύο εἰς σάρκα μίαν
verses.king_james: And said, For this cause shall a man leave father and mother, and shall cleave to his wife: and they twain shall be one flesh?

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque jam non sunt duo, sed una caro. Quod ergo Deus conjunxit, homo non separet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε οὐκέτι εἰσὶν δύο ἀλλὰ σὰρξ μία ὃ οὖν ὁ θεὸς συνέζευξεν ἄνθρωπος μὴ χωριζέτω
verses.king_james: Wherefore they are no more twain, but one flesh. What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicunt illi: Quid ergo Moyses mandavit dare libellum repudii, et dimittere?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγουσιν αὐτῷ τί οὖν μωσῆς ἐνετείλατο δοῦναι βιβλίον ἀποστασίου καὶ ἀπολῦσαι αὐτήν
verses.king_james: They say unto him, Why did Moses then command to give a writing of divorcement, and to put her away?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illis: Quoniam Moyses ad duritiam cordis vestri permisit vobis dimittere uxores vestras: ab initio autem non fuit sic.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτοῖς ὅτι μωσῆς πρὸς τὴν σκληροκαρδίαν ὑμῶν ἐπέτρεψεν ὑμῖν ἀπολῦσαι τὰς γυναῖκας ὑμῶν ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς δὲ οὐ γέγονεν οὕτως
verses.king_james: He saith unto them, Moses because of the hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your wives: but from the beginning it was not so.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico autem vobis, quia quicumque dimiserit uxorem suam, nisi ob fornicationem, et aliam duxerit, mœchatur: et qui dimissam duxerit, mœchatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω δὲ ὑμῖν ὅτι ὃς ἂν ἀπολύσῃ τὴν γυναῖκα αὐτοῦ εἰ μὴ ἐπὶ πορνείᾳ καὶ γαμήσῃ ἄλλην μοιχᾶται καὶ ὁ ἀπολελυμένην γαμήσας μοιχᾶται
verses.king_james: And I say unto you, Whosoever shall put away his wife, except *it be* for fornication, and shall marry another, committeth adultery: and whoso marrieth her which is put away doth commit adultery.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicunt ei discipuli ejus: Si ita est causa hominis cum uxore, non expedit nubere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγουσιν αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ εἰ οὕτως ἐστὶν ἡ αἰτία τοῦ ἀνθρώπου μετὰ τῆς γυναικός οὐ συμφέρει γαμῆσαι
verses.king_james: His disciples say unto him, If the case of the man be so with *his* wife, it is not good to marry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixit illis: Non omnes capiunt verbum istud, sed quibus datum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς οὐ πάντες χωροῦσιν τὸν λόγον τοῦτον ἀλλ᾽ οἷς δέδοται
verses.king_james: But he said unto them, All *men* cannot receive this saying, save *they* to whom it is given.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sunt enim eunuchi, qui de matris utero sic nati sunt: et sunt eunuchi, qui facti sunt ab hominibus: et sunt eunuchi, qui seipsos castraverunt propter regnum cælorum. Qui potest capere capiat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰσὶν γὰρ εὐνοῦχοι οἵτινες ἐκ κοιλίας μητρὸς ἐγεννήθησαν οὕτως καὶ εἰσὶν εὐνοῦχοι οἵτινες εὐνουχίσθησαν ὑπὸ τῶν ἀνθρώπων καὶ εἰσὶν εὐνοῦχοι οἵτινες εὐνούχισαν ἑαυτοὺς διὰ τὴν βασιλείαν τῶν οὐρανῶν ὁ δυνάμενος χωρεῖν χωρείτω
verses.king_james: For there are some eunuchs, which were so born from *their* mother's womb: and there are some eunuchs, which were made eunuchs of men: and there be eunuchs, which have made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heaven's sake. He that is able to receive *it*, let him receive *it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc oblati sunt ei parvuli, ut manus eis imponeret, et oraret. Discipuli autem increpabant eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε προσηνέχθη αὐτῷ παιδία ἵνα τὰς χεῖρας ἐπιθῇ αὐτοῖς καὶ προσεύξηται οἱ δὲ μαθηταὶ ἐπετίμησαν αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: Then were there brought unto him little children, that he should put *his* hands on them, and pray: and the disciples rebuked them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus vero ait eis: Sinite parvulos, et nolite eos prohibere ad me venire: talium est enim regnum cælorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν ἄφετε τὰ παιδία καὶ μὴ κωλύετε αὐτὰ ἐλθεῖν πρός με τῶν γὰρ τοιούτων ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν
verses.king_james: But Jesus said, Suffer little children, and forbid them not, to come unto me: for of such is the kingdom of heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum imposuisset eis manus, abiit inde.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπιθεὶς αὐτοῖς τὰς χεῖρας ἐπορεύθη ἐκεῖθεν
verses.king_james: And he laid *his* hands on them, and departed thence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce unus accedens, ait illi: Magister bone, quid boni faciam ut habeam vitam æternam?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού εἷς προσελθὼν εἶπεν αὐτῷ διδάσκαλε ἀγαθε τί ἀγαθὸν ποιήσω ἵνα ἔχω ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.king_james: And, behold, one came and said unto him, Good Master, what good thing shall I do, that I may have eternal life?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixit ei: Quid me interrogas de bono? Unus est bonus, Deus. Si autem vis ad vitam ingredi, serva mandata.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἰπεν αὐτῷ τί με λέγεις ἀγαθόν οὐδεὶς ἀγαθός εἰ μὴ εἷς ὁ φεός εἰ δὲ θέλεις εἰσελθεῖν εἰς τὴν ζωὴν τήρησον τὰς ἐντολάς
verses.king_james: And he said unto him, Why callest thou me good? *there is* none good but one, *that is*, God: but if thou wilt enter into life, keep the commandments.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit illi: Quæ? Jesus autem dixit: Non homicidium facies; non adulterabis; non facies furtum; non falsum testimonium dices;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ποίας ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν τὸ οὐ φονεύσεις οὐ μοιχεύσεις οὐ κλέψεις οὐ ψευδομαρτυρήσεις
verses.king_james: He saith unto him, Which? Jesus said, Thou shalt do no murder, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness,

verses.latin_vulgate: honora patrem tuum, et matrem tuam, et diliges proximum tuum sicut teipsum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίμα τὸν πατέρα σου καὶ τὴν μητέρα καί ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς σεαυτόν
verses.king_james: Honour thy father and *thy* mother: and, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit illi adolescens: Omnia hæc custodivi a juventute mea: quid adhuc mihi deest?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ νεανίσκος πάντα ταῦτα ἐφυλαξάμην ἐκ νεότητός μου τί ἔτι ὑστερῶ
verses.king_james: The young man saith unto him, All these things have I kept from my youth up: what lack I yet?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illi Jesus: Si vis perfectus esse, vade, vende quæ habes, et da pauperibus, et habebis thesaurum in cælo: et veni, sequere me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔφη αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰ θέλεις τέλειος εἶναι ὕπαγε πώλησόν σου τὰ ὑπάρχοντα καὶ δὸς πτωχοῖς καὶ ἕξεις θησαυρὸν ἐν οὐρανῷ καὶ δεῦρο ἀκολούθει μοι
verses.king_james: Jesus said unto him, If thou wilt be perfect, go *and* sell that thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come *and* follow me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum audisset autem adolescens verbum, abiit tristis: erat enim habens multas possessiones.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσας δὲ ὁ νεανίσκος τὸν λόγον ἀπῆλθεν λυπούμενος ἦν γὰρ ἔχων κτήματα πολλά
verses.king_james: But when the young man heard that saying, he went away sorrowful: for he had great possessions.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem dixit discipulis suis: Amen dico vobis, quia dives difficile intrabit in regnum cælorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι δυσκόλως πλούσιος εἰσελεύσεται εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τῶν οὐρανῶν
verses.king_james: Then said Jesus unto his disciples, Verily I say unto you, That a rich man shall hardly enter into the kingdom of heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et iterum dico vobis: Facilius est camelum per foramen acus transire, quam divitem intrare in regnum cælorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν δὲ λέγω ὑμῖν εὐκοπώτερόν ἐστιν κάμηλον διὰ τρυπήματος ῥαφίδος διελθεῖν ἢ πλούσιον εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ εἰσελθεῖν
verses.king_james: And again I say unto you, It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Auditis autem his, discipuli mirabantur valde, dicentes: Quis ergo poterit salvus esse?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσαντες δὲ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ἐξεπλήσσοντο σφόδρα λέγοντες τίς ἄρα δύναται σωθῆναι
verses.king_james: When his disciples heard *it*, they were exceedingly amazed, saying, Who then can be saved?

verses.latin_vulgate: Aspiciens autem Jesus, dixit illis: Apud homines hoc impossibile est: apud Deum autem omnia possibilia sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐμβλέψας δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς παρὰ ἀνθρώποις τοῦτο ἀδύνατόν ἐστιν παρὰ δὲ θεῷ πάντα δυνατά ἐστίν
verses.king_james: But Jesus beheld *them*, and said unto them, With men this is impossible; but with God all things are possible.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc respondens Petrus, dixit ei: Ecce nos reliquimus omnia, et secuti sumus te: quid ergo erit nobis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ πέτρος εἰπεν αὐτῷ ἰδού ἡμεῖς ἀφήκαμεν πάντα καὶ ἠκολουθήσαμέν σοι τί ἄρα ἔσται ἡμῖν
verses.king_james: Then answered Peter and said unto him, Behold, we have forsaken all, and followed thee; what shall we have therefore?

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem dixit illis: Amen dico vobis, quod vos, qui secuti estis me, in regeneratione cum sederit Filius hominis in sede majestatis suæ, sedebitis et vos super sedes duodecim, judicantes duodecim tribus Israël.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ὑμεῖς οἱ ἀκολουθήσαντές μοι ἐν τῇ παλιγγενεσίᾳ ὅταν καθίσῃ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐπὶ θρόνου δόξης αὐτοῦ καθίσεσθε καὶ ὑμεῖς ἐπὶ δώδεκα θρόνους κρίνοντες τὰς δώδεκα φυλὰς τοῦ ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: And Jesus said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of his glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omnis qui reliquerit domum, vel fratres, aut sorores, aut patrem, aut matrem, aut uxorem, aut filios, aut agros propter nomen meum, centuplum accipiet, et vitam æternam possidebit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πᾶς ὅς ἀφῆκεν οἰκίας ἢ ἀδελφοὺς ἢ ἀδελφὰς ἢ πατέρα ἢ μητέρα ἢ γυναῖκά ἢ τέκνα ἢ ἀγροὺς ἕνεκεν τοῦ ὀνόματός μου ἑκατονταπλασίονα λήψεται καὶ ζωὴν αἰώνιον κληρονομήσει
verses.king_james: And every one that hath forsaken houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my name's sake, shall receive an hundredfold, and shall inherit everlasting life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multi autem erunt primi novissimi, et novissimi primi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλοὶ δὲ ἔσονται πρῶτοι ἔσχατοι καὶ ἔσχατοι πρῶτοι
verses.king_james: But many *that are* first shall be last; and the last *shall be* first.
